# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: ci-cd

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: prod-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write

defaults:
  run:
    shell: bash

env:
  CI: true
  NODE_ENV: production

jobs:
  build_test_deploy:
    name: Build, Test, Deploy (Production)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "npm"
          cache-dependency-path: |
            package-lock.json
            **/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund

      - name: Test
        run: npm test

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Package artifacts
        run: |
          mkdir -p dist_artifacts
          if [ -d dist ]; then tar -czf dist_artifacts/dist.tgz dist; fi
          if [ -d build ]; then tar -czf dist_artifacts/build.tgz build; fi
          if [ -d .next ]; then tar -czf dist_artifacts/next.tgz .next; fi
          if [ -d public ]; then tar -czf dist_artifacts/public.tgz public; fi
          if ls dist_artifacts/*.tgz >/dev/null 2>&1; then ls -lah dist_artifacts; else echo "No recognized build output directories found."; fi

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: app-artifacts
          path: dist_artifacts
          if-no-files-found: ignore
          retention-days: 3
          compression-level: 0

      - name: Deploy to production
        env:
          DEPLOY_HOST: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_SSH_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}
          DEPLOY_PORT: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PORT }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
          DEPLOY_SCRIPT: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SCRIPT }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          : "${DEPLOY_HOST:?Missing DEPLOY_HOST secret}"
          : "${DEPLOY_USER:?Missing DEPLOY_USER secret}"
          : "${DEPLOY_SSH_KEY:?Missing DEPLOY_SSH_KEY secret}"

          DEPLOY_PORT="${DEPLOY_PORT:-22}"
          DEPLOY_PATH="${DEPLOY_PATH:-/var/www/app}"
          DEPLOY_SCRIPT="${DEPLOY_SCRIPT:-}"

          if ! ls dist_artifacts/*.tgz >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "No artifacts to deploy. Ensure build outputs are produced."
            exit 1
          fi

          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          chmod 700 ~/.ssh
          ssh-keyscan -p "$DEPLOY_PORT" -H "$DEPLOY_HOST" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null

          keyfile="$(mktemp)"
          printf "%s\n" "$DEPLOY_SSH_KEY" > "$keyfile"
          chmod 600 "$keyfile"

          ts="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
          remote_release="$DEPLOY_PATH/releases/$ts"
          remote_current="$DEPLOY_PATH/current"

          ssh -i "$keyfile" -p "$DEPLOY_PORT" -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST" "mkdir -p '$DEPLOY_PATH/releases' '$DEPLOY_PATH/shared' '$remote_release'"

          for f in dist_artifacts/*.tgz; do
            scp -i "$keyfile" -P "$DEPLOY_PORT" -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes "$f" "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST:$remote_release/"
          done

          ssh -i "$keyfile" -p "$DEPLOY_PORT" -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST" "cd '$remote_release' && for a in ./*.tgz; do tar -xzf \"\$a\"; done && rm -f ./*.tgz"

          ssh -i "$keyfile" -p "$DEPLOY_PORT" -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST" "ln -sfn '$remote_release' '$remote_current'"

          if [ -n "$DEPLOY_SCRIPT" ]; then
            ssh -i "$keyfile" -p "$DEPLOY_PORT" -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST" "cd '$remote_current' && $DEPLOY_SCRIPT"
          fi

          rm -f "$keyfile"
